description: Per-control rules for mapping vulnerability findings to compliance controls. Each rule defines a predicate that is evaluated against a vulnerability input. Multiple rules can match the same vuln (a vuln can map to multiple controls). Predicates are pure data, evaluated by the mapping-engine.

rules.title: Continuous Vulnerability Management
rules.description: Any CVE with severity >= Medium is in scope of CIS Control 7 (Continuous Vulnerability Management).

rules.title: Application Software Security
rules.description: Any CVE discovered through Software Composition Analysis (SCA) is in scope of CIS Control 16 (Application Software Security).

rules.title: Flaw Remediation
rules.description: All CVEs are subject to NIST SI-2 (Flaw Remediation). This is the default catch-all rule.

rules.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
rules.description: All CVEs detected by the platform's scanning are evidence of NIST RA-5 (Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning) operating.

rules.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
rules.description: CVEs flagged as Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) — actively exploited in the wild — escalate to NIST SI-7 (Software/Firmware Integrity) for integrity-verification and emergency remediation.

rules.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation
rules.description: CVEs introduced into the codebase within the last 30 days imply a gap in NIST SA-11 (Developer Testing and Evaluation) — pre-commit and CI tests should have caught them.
